communities.title: P2P Builders
communities.audience: Pear, Holepunch, local-first, and decentralized app builders.
communities.founderBrief: Seed practical build logs, protocol questions, and demos. Keep posts concrete and source-linked.

communities.title: Nostr
communities.audience: Nostr users, relays, client builders, and censorship-resistant social users.
communities.launchRole: Best ideological fit for the no-server social pitch.
communities.founderBrief: Compare relay-based social networks with P2P outboxes without turning the board into token promotion.

communities.title: Self-Hosted
communities.audience: People who run home servers, NAS boxes, personal cloud, and homelab services.
communities.launchRole: Practical operator funnel for future HiveRelay nodes.
communities.founderBrief: Focus on durable ownership, migration away from SaaS, and what should run at home versus on relays.

communities.title: Privacy
communities.audience: PrivacyGuides, degoogle, Tor, Brave, and no-account software users.
communities.launchRole: Core message fit for no-account posting and local identity.
communities.founderBrief: Keep claims precise: no central account, signed identities, public boards, and no magic anonymity promises.

communities.title: Linux
communities.audience: Linux desktop, FOSS, package maintainers, and power users.
communities.founderBrief: Seed posts about PearBrowser support, distro packaging, and desktop P2P UX.

communities.title: Local-First
communities.audience: People building sync-first, offline-capable, user-owned software.
communities.founderBrief: Bring rigorous comparisons: CRDTs, Hypercore, sync UX, offline trust, and recovery.

communities.title: Local AI
communities.audience: Ollama, llama.cpp, MLX, homelab GPU, and local model users.
communities.launchRole: Bridge to future HiveCompute and GPU marketplace demand.
communities.founderBrief: Seed practical benchmarks, model notes, and local inference workflows. Avoid hype-only posts.

communities.title: Fediverse
communities.audience: Lemmy, Mastodon, Mbin, and federation users.
communities.founderBrief: Frame Peerit as a no-server experiment, not a replacement war. Invite bridge ideas.

communities.title: Cypherpunk
communities.audience: Bitcoin, Lightning, privacy engineering, and censorship-resistance builders.
communities.launchRole: Narrative engine for signed records and PoW-gated posting.
communities.founderBrief: Keep discussion technical and lawful. No darknet-market positioning.

communities.title: Reddit Alternatives
communities.audience: People actively looking for community platforms outside Reddit.
communities.founderBrief: Host comparisons, migration checklists, and honest limits of P2P community software.

communities.title: Homelab
communities.audience: Operators who may eventually run relays, seeders, and compute nodes.
communities.founderBrief: Seed posts about always-on peers, storage proofs, monitoring, and low-power nodes.

communities.title: HiveRelay
communities.audience: Relay operators, node-curious users, seeders, and people who want to make P2P apps reliably available.
communities.launchRole: Infrastructure-as-growth channel for Peerit and PearBrowser.
communities.founderBrief: Seed posts about running nodes, proof receipts, app/outbox seeding, referral links, regional coverage, and operator rewards.

communities.title: Showcase
communities.audience: People launching demos, tools, boards, bots, and importers.
communities.founderBrief: Keep this useful: demos, screenshots, source links, and feedback requests.
